feedback.text: Dear European Commission, To consider, I submit two points to the Commission for feedback on this Bill. 1 – Shortback to private and foreign influence over EU citizens Companies such as Visa and Mastercard should not be able to condition access to legal commerce by private logging groups from non-EU countries. Denial of arbitrary and subjective service should be considered an unfair commercial practice; it should require objective criteria from official governmental organisations within the European Union. The EU should consider the creation of a payment processor to the public. Visa and Mastercard are large corporations; if anyone can compete with them, it is an EU payment processor. These payment processors are a daily necessity; it is strategically important for us to have strong domestic exchanges. My example here under Visa and Mastercard is only one example. We need stronger laws to prevent foreign influence across the field. The practice in which countries use robotic accounts to shape public opinion on social websites is also an unrealistic and dangerous threat. 2 – Refute censorship of legal speech (consumer fairness) Consent with ideas is not valid to suppress them. People should be free to express lawful opinions, even those I strongly stand against. Within this Act, I appeal to the Commission to consider preventing businesses from making access to digital services subject to an agreed condition with a lawful attitude, and to require that any restriction be based solely on the law, and not on private enforcement. Thank you for considering this feedback. I'm to respect
